title: ATOMIC: Delete Ruby/Elixir/OCaml regex banks + raw-fallback dups + 6 obsolete unit tests

regex_bank_deletions.RUBY_PATTERNS.sources: Deleted 4 of 5 regex entries (STDIN.{read,gets,readline}, params[, ENV[, File.{read,open}). RETAINED: \bgets\b UserInput entry per Option A (M1 finding #2 carry-forward). Tree-sitter-ruby parses bare `gets` as identifier, not call, so AST `call_names: ['gets']` does not fire on bare form. Documented in source comment + commit message.
regex_bank_deletions.RUBY_PATTERNS.sinks: Deleted all 4 regex entries (eval, system|exec, IO.popen, .send). KEEP sanitizers.
regex_bank_deletions.ELIXIR_PATTERNS.sources: Deleted all 3 regex entries (IO.gets, System.get_env, File.{read,read!}). KEEP sanitizers.
regex_bank_deletions.ELIXIR_PATTERNS.sinks: Deleted all 3 regex entries (System.cmd, Code.eval_string, Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query). KEEP sanitizers.
regex_bank_deletions.OCAML_PATTERNS.sources: Deleted all 4 regex entries (read_line, Sys.getenv, input_line, In_channel.{read_all,input_all}). KEEP sanitizers.
regex_bank_deletions.OCAML_PATTERNS.sinks: Deleted all 3 regex entries (Sys.command, Unix.execvp, Sqlite3.exec). KEEP sanitizers.

raw_fallback_ast_deletions.RUBY_AST_SOURCES: Deleted ('', 'STDIN.read'), ('', 'STDIN.gets'), ('', 'STDIN.readline'), ('', 'File.read'), ('', 'File.open'). KEPT structured ('STDIN', read|gets|readline) + ('File', read|open). KEPT subscripts ('', 'params['), ('', 'ENV[').

comment_updates: Ruby PARTIAL coverage comment at L2336 — past-tense rewrite (M5 deletion done)
Elixir PARTIAL coverage comment at L2766 — past-tense rewrite
OCaml PARTIAL coverage comment at L2828 — past-tense rewrite
compute_taint dispatch HOLD comment at L3970 — removed HOLD reference, documented \bgets\b retention exception

carry_forward_fixes.ruby_ast_sources_reordering: Stdin pattern moved BEFORE UserInput pattern. The UserInput call_names: ['gets'] entry uses ends_with('.gets') heuristic which would otherwise shadow STDIN.gets and lose the more-specific Stdin source type. Pre-M5, regex bank produced both; post-M5 only the most-specific structured AST entry should fire per descendant.
carry_forward_fixes.extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_ocaml: Extended to handle OCaml application_expression — child(0) is the function, child(1..) are arguments. Pre-M5 the regex bank's `extract_call_arg` text-scanned past the function name; post-M5 we need an AST equivalent. Strips parens around (expr) parenthesised args.
carry_forward_fixes.detect_sources_ast_var_fallback: Added `extract_source_var_from_statement` text-fallback + synthetic-var-from-call-name fallback. For call-shaped sources whose only arguments are string literals (Elixir `IO.gets("> ")` in a pipe chain, OCaml `Sys.getenv "VAR"`), neither parent-assignment nor first-arg extraction yields a var. Without this, AST-detected sources would be silently dropped post-regex-deletion. Pre-M5 the regex path produced a TaintSource (with synthetic var) so source-type assertions in integration tests succeed.
carry_forward_fixes.detect_sinks_ast_synthetic_var_fallback: Mirror of source fallback: when call-shaped sinks have non-identifier args (`System.cmd([])` with list literal), derive synthetic var from leading identifier of call_name.

tests.exit_code_rationale: 1 due to 20 pre-existing orthogonal failures (kotlin doctest, luau doctest, surface/triggers doctest, FuncIndexProxy doctest, p2_language_status, tree_handles_empty_directory, etc.) — ALL present at M4 HEAD too. ZERO M5-induced regressions. The only M5-related failure is `ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint` ast_only-path which was ALREADY FAILING at M4 (M1 finding #2: bare `gets` parses as identifier in tree-sitter-ruby — known carry-forward gap untouched by M5).
tests.module_function_integ_tests: 20/21 PASS in rr_module_function_integ_test.rs. The 21st (ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint ast_only) is the carry-forward known failure documented in M4-report.json.
tests.baseline_per_language_tests: All 19 pass in rr_baseline_per_language_test.rs including the 3 field_access_info-extension-v1 baseline tests (Ruby/Elixir/OCaml Module.function shape end-to-end via analyze_with_ssa).

binary_smoke_string_literal_zero_finding.ruby: ZERO sources/sinks for `comment = "use IO.popen for shell exec"` fixture — closes-#24 generalized to Ruby
binary_smoke_string_literal_zero_finding.elixir: ZERO sources/sinks for `comment = "remember to use System.cmd here"` fixture — closes-#24 generalized to Elixir
binary_smoke_string_literal_zero_finding.ocaml: ZERO sources/sinks for `let comment = "Sys.command runs the shell"` fixture — closes-#24 generalized to OCaml

binary_smoke_real_detection.ruby: STDIN.gets -> Stdin source ✓, IO.popen(cmd) -> ShellExec sink ✓, TAINTED flow detected ✓
binary_smoke_real_detection.ocaml: Sys.getenv -> EnvVar source ✓, Sys.command cmd -> ShellExec sink ✓, TAINTED flow detected ✓

checks.subscript_regression: params[:name] -> HttpParam ✓, ENV['SECRET_KEY'] -> EnvVar ✓, both flow correctly to system(name) ShellExec sink

discoveries.option_choice: Option A — KEEP \bgets\b regex in RUBY_PATTERNS.sources
discoveries.option_rationale: Tree-sitter-ruby parses bare `gets` (without parens, not as method call on a receiver) as an `identifier` node, not a `call` or `method_call` node. So `call_names: ['gets']` in RUBY_AST_SOURCES does NOT fire on the bare form. Option C (extracting bare gets via extract_call_name_ruby) was forbidden by validator mandate `no_field_access_info_source_change`. Option A is simplest and preserves the M5 atomicity. Documented as carry-forward exception in source code comment, dispatch comment, and commit message.
discoveries.ruby_ast_pattern_ordering: Discovered ordering bug exposed by deletion: UserInput call_names ['gets'] uses ends_with('.gets') which shadowed the more-specific Stdin ('STDIN', 'gets') member_pattern. Pre-M5, the regex bank produced BOTH UserInput and Stdin from the same line (both match independently). Post-M5, the AST loop's `break;` after first match means only the FIRST pattern fires per descendant. Fixed by reordering — Stdin patterns BEFORE UserInput in RUBY_AST_SOURCES.
discoveries.ocaml_application_expression_var_extract: OCaml application_expression has no `arguments` field — child(0) is the function expression and child(1..) are the arguments. The pre-M5 W2-pre `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast` did not handle this and returned None. Pre-M5 the regex bank's `extract_call_arg` text-scanned past the function name. Added OCaml-specific branch.
discoveries.string_literal_only_args_var_dropping: For sources/sinks where ALL arguments are string literals or non-identifier expressions (Elixir `IO.gets("> ")` in pipe, OCaml `Sys.getenv "CMD"`, Elixir `System.cmd([])` with list literal), AST var extraction returned None and the source/sink was silently dropped. Pre-M5 the regex bank's `extract_source_var_from_statement` produced a synthetic non-None var (typically the next call's leading identifier from text scan). Replicated this with text-fallback + final synthetic-var-from-call-name fallback. Var is synthetic but sufficient for integration test source-type/sink-type assertions.
discoveries.string_literal_regression_guards_auto_fi: Per M1 finding #2 prediction: `elixir_string_literal_system_cmd_substring_zero_findings` and `ocaml_string_literal_sys_command_substring_zero_findings` (RED at HEAD pre-M5 due to substring matches in raw-fallback `("", "X.y")` and permissive `Sys\.command\s` regex matching inside string literals' descendant text) became GREEN at M5 — deleting raw-fallback duplicates + the OCaml `Sys\.command\s` regex closes the substring leak. Confirmed in M5-capture.txt + binary smoke.

loc_delta_actual: -278 (taint.rs +281/-167 = +114 net additions counting carry-forward fixes; taint_tests.rs -331/-114 = net -217 from test deletions; combined -278 (167-445))
loc_delta_contract: -215 (loose match — extra LOC in carry-forward fixes for var extraction)

compliance.no_field_access_info_source_change: Confirmed — no edits to ast_utils.rs field_access_info or extract_call_name_*. Carry-forward fixes are confined to taint.rs's extract_first_identifier_arg_ast, detect_sources_ast, detect_sinks_ast, and pattern bank ordering.
compliance.no_sanitizer_bank_change: Confirmed — all 16 sanitizer regex banks retained pending sanitizer-removal-v1.
